topic.title_cn: 描述一次尴尬或不适经历
topic.prompt_en: Describe an awkward or uncomfortable experience.
topic.learning_goal: Learn to tell an awkward moment lightly, without overacting or sounding childish.

angles.title: Set the scene
content: Say when it happened and why it mattered at the time.
Keep the background short so you can move into the event quickly.

angles.title: Explain what happened
content: Describe the main action in a clear time order.
Choose one or two details that make the event believable.

angles.title: Show the turning point
content: Include the moment when the situation changed or became clear.
That moment usually carries the emotional weight of the answer.

angles.title: End with meaning
content: Finish with the lesson, result, or lasting impression.
A short reflection gives the story a real ending.

sequence.target: State the situation, time, and why this event belongs in your answer.
Describe what happened and where the key shift came.
End with the effect, lesson, or memory that stayed with you.

high_score_tips: Keep the event focused on one clear thread instead of listing everything that happened.
Use one turning point so the answer has shape and forward movement.
A calm reflection usually sounds stronger than exaggerated emotion.
Let the ending show why the event still matters to you now.

content.why_it_hurts: The listener loses the main story if every small detail gets equal time.
content.fix: Keep only the details that change the meaning or the outcome.

content.why_it_hurts: Without a shift, the answer sounds like a flat report instead of a real experience.
content.fix: Identify the exact moment when something became easier, harder, clearer, or more memorable.

content.why_it_hurts: The story feels unfinished if you stop right after the event ends.
content.fix: Add one sentence about what you learned or how you see it now.

content.why_it_hurts: The response sounds memorised if it stays at the level of general ideas and never lands on one lived detail.
content.fix: Add one concrete moment, reaction, or small consequence that actually happened to you.

content.problem: Reflection that only repeats the opening
content.why_it_hurts: The ending feels flat if it only restates the topic instead of adding insight or change.
content.fix: Use the final lines to show what changed, what you realised, or how you would respond now.

language.wrong_example: I go there last week and everyone is waiting for me.
language.better_example: I went there last week and everyone was waiting for me.
language.reason: When you tell a finished event, simple past should carry the main timeline.

language.wrong_example: It was very memorable and very special.
language.better_example: It was memorable because it changed how I handled similar situations later.
language.reason: Specific explanation is stronger than repeating very.

language.problem: Overusing very or really with basic adjectives
language.wrong_example: It was very good and really important for me.
language.better_example: It turned out to be more meaningful than I expected.
language.reason: A precise evaluation sounds more natural than stacking intensifiers on basic adjectives.

language.problem: Repeating I think in every sentence
language.wrong_example: I think it is useful, and I think it matters because I think it affects daily life.
language.better_example: What makes it useful to me is the way it affects daily life.
language.reason: Removing repeated I think makes the answer sound more confident and more fluent.

core.example: I would naturally use "incident" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "outcome" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "detail" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "reaction" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "awkward" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "muted" when talking about this topic.

extended.note: Useful when the story changes direction.

core.example: I would use "what happened was" when describing an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context.
I would use "at first" when describing an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context.
I would use "in the end" when describing an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context.
I would use "it taught me that" when describing an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context.
I would use "feel awkward" when describing an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context.
I would use "move on quickly" when describing an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context.

extended.phrase: one thing that stood out was
extended.note: Good for the moment you want to emphasize.
extended.example: I would use "one thing that stood out was" when describing an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context.

extended.note: Useful when the event caused a later change.
extended.example: I would use "from that point on" when describing an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context.
I would use "take it too seriously" when describing an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context.
I would use "realise it too late" when describing an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context.

framework.goal: Tell one event clearly through context, turning point, result, and reflection.

moves: Set the time and situation in one or two sentences.
Show why this event was worth talking about.
Make the turning point obvious and easy to follow.
State the outcome and what stayed with you.
Keep the reflection grounded and realistic.

delivery_markers: The moment that really stood out was ...
At first, I thought ... but then ...

samples.answer:
An awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context is something I can talk about quite naturally. A while ago, I joined an online meeting and spoke for nearly a minute before realising that my microphone was muted. At first, I thought people were simply being quiet, but then I noticed the chat box filling up with messages trying to get my attention. The awkward part was not the mistake itself but the few seconds when I realised everyone had been watching me talk to myself. After that, I laughed, restarted the point, and the meeting moved on quite quickly.

What made it worth talking about was not only the event or idea itself, but also the effect it had on the way I think or act. It was embarrassing for the moment, but it also reminded me not to take small public mistakes too seriously. Overall, it is a topic that feels real to me because it connects to ordinary life rather than a perfect story.
highlights: Band 6 answer matched to the 描述一次尴尬或不适经历 topic
Keeps spoken rhythm while staying specific and teachable

opening: The topic that comes to mind is an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context.
I would first explain the background clearly.
body: Then I would add the central detail or turning point.
After that, I would show the result or practical effect.
closing: Finally, I would end with the takeaway or present view.

upgrades.improved: It was embarrassing for the moment, but it also reminded me not to take small public mistakes too seriously.
upgrades.why: A specific reason sounds much more natural than a general claim.
upgrades.note: Use the personal effect to close the answer.

upgrades.improved: The awkward part was not the mistake itself but the few seconds when I realised everyone had been watching me talk to myself.
upgrades.why: A clear turning point gives the answer shape and movement.
upgrades.note: This is especially useful in spoken storytelling and reflective answers.

upgrades.original: It was very important to me.
upgrades.improved: What made it important was the effect it had on the way I handled similar situations later.
upgrades.why: This explains importance through a concrete effect instead of a vague adjective.
upgrades.note: Useful when you want a simple but natural reflective sentence.

upgrades.original: I learned a lot from it.
upgrades.improved: I came away from it with a clearer idea of what I would do next time.
upgrades.why: This sounds more spoken and more specific than a flat learned a lot.
upgrades.note: Good for endings that focus on takeaway and future behaviour.

samples.answer:
The topic that comes to mind for me is an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context. A while ago, I joined an online meeting and spoke for nearly a minute before realising that my microphone was muted. At first, I thought people were simply being quiet, but then I noticed the chat box filling up with messages trying to get my attention. What stood out most was that the awkward part was not the mistake itself but the few seconds when I realised everyone had been watching me talk to myself.

After that, after that, I laughed, restarted the point, and the meeting moved on quite quickly. What I find interesting about it now is that the situation seemed fairly ordinary at the time, but it revealed something more lasting about how I handle pressure, choices, or relationships. It was embarrassing for the moment, but it also reminded me not to take small public mistakes too seriously. So if I were explaining this in a speaking test, I would focus less on sounding dramatic and more on why the experience or idea genuinely stayed with me.
highlights: Band 7 answer matched to the 描述一次尴尬或不适经历 topic
Keeps spoken rhythm while staying specific and teachable

band_guide.focus: More detail and stronger turning point
opening: The topic that comes to mind is an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context.

body: Then I would add the central detail or turning point.

closing: Finally, I would end with the takeaway or present view.

upgrades.improved: The awkward part was not the mistake itself but the few seconds when I realised everyone had been watching me talk to myself.

upgrades.note: This is especially useful in spoken storytelling and reflective answers.

upgrades.improved: What helped most was that it changed the way I judged the situation in practice.
upgrades.why: This sounds more mature and more natural than the vague phrase helped me a lot.
upgrades.note: Useful when you want to show growth without sounding dramatic.

upgrades.improved: It gave me a more balanced way to look at similar situations afterwards.
upgrades.why: Balanced way to look at is a more idiomatic reflective phrase in spoken English.
upgrades.note: Works well in opinion, process, and experience answers.

samples.answer:
If I had to answer this topic in a more reflective way, I would still begin with an awkward moment that taught me to pay attention to context. A while ago, I joined an online meeting and spoke for nearly a minute before realising that my microphone was muted. At first, I thought people were simply being quiet, but then I noticed the chat box filling up with messages trying to get my attention. The turning point, for me, was that the awkward part was not the mistake itself but the few seconds when I realised everyone had been watching me talk to myself.

From there, after that, I laughed, restarted the point, and the meeting moved on quite quickly. What matters now is not the surface event or statement alone, but the shift in perspective behind it. It was embarrassing for the moment, but it also reminded me not to take small public mistakes too seriously. In that sense, the topic feels useful because it shows not only what happened or what I think, but also how my judgment has become a little more precise over time.

practice.task: Describe an awkward or uncomfortable experience.
checklist: Set the scene briefly before the main event.
End with the result or lesson, not just the action.
self_prompts: What changed in the middle of the story?
Why do you still remember it now?
What did you learn from it?
